title: DataSHIELD Client Functions for Vertically Partitioned Data
text: dsVertClient provides user-friendly R functions privacy-preserving analysis vertically partitioned data across DataSHIELD servers. analyst calls simple functions; cryptographic protocols (ECDH-PSI, Ring63 / Ring127 Beaver MPC, DCF wide-spline, OT-Beaver dishonest-majority triples, X25519 + AES-256-GCM transport, Ed25519 identity verification) run transparently. Pair server-side companion package dsVert.

title: DataSHIELD Client Functions for Vertically Partitioned Data
code: library(DSI); library(DSOpal); library(dsVertClient)  # Connect to servers conns <- DSI::datashield.login(logins = builder$build()) DSI::datashield.assign.table(conns, "D", "project.data")  # 1. Align records (NAs removed automatically, like glm na.omit) ds.psiAlign("D", "patient_id", "DA", datasources = conns)  # 2. Fit a federated GLM (auto-detects which server has each variable) fit <- ds.vertGLM(diabetes ~ age + bmi + glu + bp,                   data = "DA", family = "binomial",                   datasources = conns) print(fit)  # 3. Cox PH via the non-disclosive Breslow profile route cox <- ds.vertCoxProfileNonDisclosive(   survival::Surv(time, event) ~ age + bmi + bp,   data = "DA",   datasources = conns )  # 4. Correlation + PCA cor <- ds.vertCor("DA", datasources = conns) pca <- ds.vertPCA(cor_result = cor)  DSI::datashield.logout(conns)

title: Adaptive Chunking for DataSHIELD Transport — chunk-utils
text: Utilities sending large payloads DataSHIELD's R expression parser automatic chunk size detection fallback.

title: Adaptive Chunking for DataSHIELD Transport — chunk-utils
text: DataSHIELD passes function arguments inline R expressions via HTTP. Large string arguments (base64-encoded ciphertexts, EC points, encrypted vectors) can exceed parser HTTP body size limit. module implements adaptive chunking: Start large initial chunk size (default 100KB, configurable via options(dsvert.chunk_size = N)) first large send, probe whether chunk size accepted probe fails, reduce chunk size 25\ Cache successful chunk size subsequent sends probe conclusive chunk least half target chunk size successfully sent. Small payloads succeed trivially confirm full chunk size accepted.

title: LMM closed-form GLS driver — .ds_vertLMM_closed_form
text: Computes exact Laird-Ware generalised-least-squares estimate random-intercept LMM without relying ds.vertGLM's L-BFGS iteration. Directly assembles Gram matrix X'X right-hand side X'y cluster-mean- centred design via: - local per-server block computations - Beaver vecmul + FP-sum cross-server entries solves beta = solve(XtX, Xty) client-side. Matches lme4::lmer FP precision (~1e-5 small n). Assumptions: - Cluster IDs broadcast via dsvertLMMBroadcastClusterIDsDS / dsvertLMMReceiveClusterIDsDS. - Transport keys initialised session. - Exactly K=2 servers (outcome + one peer).

title: LMM closed-form GLS driver — .ds_vertLMM_closed_form

text: function performs privacy-preserving record alignment single call, using ECDH-PSI blind-relay transport encryption.

title: ECDH-PSI Record Alignment (Blind Relay) — ds.psiAlign
text: ECDH-PSI exploits commutativity elliptic curve scalar multiplication: \(\alpha \cdot (\beta \cdot H(id)) = \beta \cdot (\alpha \cdot H(id))\). EC point exchanges encrypted server--server (X25519 + AES-256-GCM ECIES). client acts blind relay, seeing opaque blobs. Phase 0: server generates X25519 transport keypair. Public keys exchanged via client. Phase 1: reference server masks IDs scalar \(\alpha\). Points stored server-side (returned client). target server: reference encrypts masked points target's PK. target decrypts, generates scalar \(\beta\), double-masks ref points (stores locally), masks IDs, encrypts ref's PK. reference decrypts, double-masks \(\alpha\), encrypts result target's PK. target decrypts, matches double-masked sets, aligns data. multi-server intersection ensures records present servers retained.

what: Security (DDH assumption on P-256, malicious-client model)
title: ECDH-PSI Record Alignment (Blind Relay) — ds.psiAlign
text: client sees opaque encrypted blobs – EC points. server's scalar never leaves server. PSI firewall: phase ordering + one-shot semantics prevent OPRF oracle attacks.

title: Cox discrete-time pooled-logistic – non-disclosive option B (#D') — ds.vertCoxDiscreteNonDisclosive

Closes disclosure gap leaky variant run_cox_discrete (person-period frame leaked J_i = #replicas(X_i) covariate server). Architecture (selected David 2026-04-27 ~10:00, option B project_k2_strict_unified_plan_2026-04-27.md "Option B FEASIBILITY ANALYSIS" Sec.): Outcome (label) server holds (time, status) -> computes J_i + m_ij = (j <= J_i) + y_ij = (j == J_i status_i = 1) plaintext. m y split Ring127 additive shares (frac=50) OS selected fusion/NL DCF party. fusion party never sees J_i directly – uniform-random-looking length-(J*n) shares whose mod-2^127 sum OS shares reconstructs masks. feature server replicates X_i uniform Jxn person-period frame (every patient contributes exactly J rows, regardless true J_i). K>=3 non-DCF servers export encrypted additive shares uniform frame. Zero row-count signal leaks per Aliasgari-Blanton 2013 NDSS share-mask gating folklore. Newton inner loop: mask-gated residual (y - p) * m via .ring127_vecmul Beaver round per bin + mask-gated Hessian X^T diag(W * m) X share-space + ridge epsilon*(~1e-8) Hessian diagonal handle -zero-mask alpha_j strata per Christensen 2019 Sec..3. masked-Newton loop runs exactly two DCF parties. K=2 parties two servers. K>=3 outcome server one selected fusion server hold DCF state; additional feature servers export encrypted additive shares uniform person-period covariate frame. Citations: Aliasgari & Blanton 2013 NDSS (eprint 2012/405) – share-mask gating FP branch elimination, underlying primitive. De Cock et al. 2016 (eprint 2016/736) – oblivious selection additive secret sharing. Mohassel & Zhang 2017 IEEE S&P (eprint 2017/396) SecureML – probabilistic-truncation noise model bounds Ring127 per-mult error budget. Catrina & Saxena 2010 FC Sec.3.3 – fixed-point representation statistical-security analysis (kappa = ring_bits - 2*frac). Andreux et al. 2020 arXiv:2006.08997 – discrete-time Cox via pooled-logistic; algorithmic substrate. Allison 1982 Sociological Methodology 13:61-98 – canonical pooled-logistic equivalence discrete Cox. Prentice & Gloeckler 1978 Biometrics 34:57-67 – discrete Cox MLE properties. Christensen 2019 CRAN ordinal vignette Sec..3 – Newton diagonal eigenvalue inflation handling singular H.

what: Proper LASSO via client-side proximal gradient on normal equations — ds.vertLASSOProximal
title: Proper LASSO via client-side proximal gradient on normal equations — ds.vertLASSOProximal
text: Cierra el gap OLS-soft-threshold -> proper-LASSO usando solo cantidades ya expuestas por ds.vertGLM (beta, covariance, n), sin anadir ninguna ronda MPC adicional. Normal equations: para Gaussian \(y = X\beta + \varepsilon\) el minimizador LASSO es $$\beta^* = \arg\min_{\beta} \tfrac{1}{2n} \|y - X\beta\|^2 + \lambda \|\beta\|_1.$$ El gradiente de la parte cuadratica es $$\nabla f(\beta) = \tfrac{1}{n} X^\top X \cdot (\beta - \hat\beta_{OLS}).$$ ds.vertGLM ya expone \(\hat\beta_{OLS}\) y \(\mathrm{Cov}(\hat\beta) = \sigma^2 (X^\top X)^{-1}\). De ahi el cliente reconstruye \(X^\top X / n = \mathrm{Cov}^{-1} \cdot \hat\sigma^2 / n\) e itera proximal-gradient puramente en memoria: \(\beta_{t+1} = S_{\lambda/L}(\beta_t - \eta \nabla f(\beta_t))\) con \(L \ge \lambda_{\max}(X^\top X / n)\) (upper bound local) y \(S_t(x) = \mathrm{sign}(x) \max(|x|-t, 0)\) el operador soft-threshold.

title: Federated linear mixed model with a single random intercept — ds.vertLMM

what: Federated multiple imputation with Rubin pooling — ds.vertMI
title: Federated multiple imputation with Rubin pooling — ds.vertMI
text: Fit GLM vertically partitioned DataSHIELD data multiple imputation missing values. Imputations stay server holding missing variable (via dsvertImputeColumnDS); client ever sees \(M\) pooled coefficient vectors covariance matrices applies Rubin's rules client-side. Protocol \(m = 1..M\): 1. every server holding column missingness, call dsvertImputeColumnDS seed \(s_m\). server draws local imputation using Bayesian-ridge model conditional complete-case columns available server. imputed column written back aligned data frame per-round name (e.g. __dsvert_imp_<var>_<m>). 2. Run ds.vertGLM imputed data, collect beta_m Cov(beta_m). 3. Client accumulates \((\beta_m, \mathrm{Cov}_m)\). Rubin's pooling rules applied client-side: $$\bar\beta = \frac{1}{M}\sum_m \beta_m$$ $$W = \frac{1}{M}\sum_m \mathrm{Cov}_m$$ $$B = \frac{1}{M-1} \sum_m (\beta_m-\bar\beta)(\beta_m-\bar\beta)^T$$ $$T = W + (1 + 1/M) B$$

what: Federated negative binomial regression with dispersion estimate — ds.vertNB
title: Federated negative binomial regression with dispersion estimate — ds.vertNB
text: Fit negative binomial GLM vertically partitioned DataSHIELD data. Two-stage: Fit \(\hat\beta\) via dsVert Poisson GLM (identical score canonical log link point estimate ; covariance differs). Estimate \(\hat\theta\) client-side Newton-Raphson NB profile log-likelihood, evaluated candidate \(\theta\) dsvertNBProfileSumsDS returns \(\sum\psi(y_i+\theta)\), \(\sum\psi_1(y_i+\theta)\), \(n\), \(\bar y\) scalar aggregates outcome server. Anscombe/Lawless score \(s(\theta)=\sum\psi(y_i+\theta)-n\psi(\theta)+n\log(\theta/(\bar y+\theta))\) zero MLE; derivative \(s'(\theta)=\sum\psi_1(y_i+\theta)-n\psi_1(\theta)+n[1/\theta-1/(\bar y+\theta)]\) supplies Newton step. Initial value comes method--moments \(\hat\theta_0=\bar y^2/(s_y^2-\bar y)\). Rescale Poisson SE \(\sqrt{1 + \bar y / \hat\theta}\) reported z-stats reflect NB variance inflation. aggregates scalar sums y; per-patient disclosure.

title: Federated NB regression with full-regression theta refinement — ds.vertNBFullRegTheta
text: NB(mu_i, theta) log-likelihood score theta $$s(\theta) = \sum_i \psi(y_i + \theta) - n \psi(\theta)                      + n \log \theta - \sum_i \log(\mu_i + \theta).$$ iid-mu approximation used ds.vertNB replaces last term \(n \log(\bar y + \theta)\). homogeneous cohorts (small \(\text{Var}(\mu)\)) tight; regression-rich settings bias theta can reach ~16% (quine, overdispersed counts) relative MASS::glm.nb. first-order correction uses aggregate marginal variance y decomposed via NB law total variance: \(\text{Var}(y) = E[\mu] + E[\mu^2]/\theta + \text{Var}(\mu)\). \(\bar y\) \(s_y^2\) (scalar aggregates dsvertLocalMomentsDS) iid theta_0 seed, refine via Brent root-finding corrected score \(s_{\text{corr}}(\theta) = s_{\text{iid}}(\theta) - \frac{1}{2}         \frac{n \, \hat{V}_\mu}{(\bar y + \theta)^2}\) \(\hat V_\mu\) aggregate estimate \(\text{Var}(\mu)\) second term Taylor correction \(\sum_i \log(\mu_i + \theta)\) around \(\bar y\). aggregate \(\hat V_\mu\) computed \(\hat V_\mu = \max(0,\; s_y^2 - \bar y - \bar y^2 / \hat\theta_0)\) – portion total y variance explained NB conditional variance \(\mu + \mu^2/\theta\). quantities scalar aggregates; per-patient disclosure. Full-share-space Clenshaw evaluation \(\sum_i \log(\mu_i + \theta)\) using shipped Ring127LogShiftPlaintext Chebyshev primitive + DCF argument reduction stricter variant scheduled separately; first-order correction closes bulk iid-mu bias (quine: 16% -> 4-5%) without new MPC machinery.

title: Federated ordinal logistic regression — ds.vertOrdinal
text: ds.vertOrdinal object (among fields): thresholds \(\alpha_k\) (intercepts K-1 cumulative binomial fits) beta_po \(\gamma\) (BLUE-pooled slope coefficients K-1 fits). CUMULATIVE-BINOMIAL GLM convention, .e.\ fit form
code: \eqn{P(Y \leq k | X) = \mathrm{sigmoid}(\alpha_k + X^\top \gamma)},  NOT the \code{MASS::polr} convention \eqn{P(Y \leq k | X) = \mathrm{sigmoid}(\theta_k - X^\top \beta)}. The two agree under \eqn{\theta_k = \alpha_k} and \eqn{\beta = -\gamma}. Therefore a caller comparing against \code{coef(polr)} must flip the sign of \code{beta_po} (or equivalently evaluate predictions with \eqn{\mathrm{sigmoid}(\theta_k + X^\top \gamma)} on the \code{ds.vertOrdinal} outputs). Empirically the cumulative probabilities agree with polr to max \eqn{|\Delta P| \approx 5 \times 10^{-2}} on the housing subset once the convention is honoured (probe_ordinal_harness.R, 2026-04-21).

what: Federated joint proportional-odds ordinal regression via Ring127 MPC-orchestrated Newton iteration — ds.vertOrdinalJointNewton
title: Federated joint proportional-odds ordinal regression via Ring127 MPC-orchestrated Newton iteration — ds.vertOrdinalJointNewton
text: Strict K=2 share-domain proportional-odds Newton route default. Patient-level linear predictors, class probabilities, reciprocals, score terms remain Ring127 shares; client receives guarded class counts, aggregate score/Hessian probes, final model parameters. older per-patient reconstruction route removed product package. K-level ordered outcome PO log-likelihood $$\ell(\beta, \theta) = \sum_i \log[F(\theta_{y_i} - \eta_i)                                            - F(\theta_{y_i-1} - \eta_i)],$$ \(F = \mathrm{sigmoid}\), \(\eta_i = X_i \beta\), \(\theta_0 = -\infty\), \(\theta_K = +\infty\). Score: $$\partial \ell / \partial \beta_j = -\sum_i x_{ij} \cdot           \frac{f(\theta_{y_i}-\eta_i) - f(\theta_{y_i-1}-\eta_i)}                {F(\theta_{y_i}-\eta_i) - F(\theta_{y_i-1}-\eta_i)},$$ \(f(u) = F(u)(1-F(u))\). MPC pipeline per outer Newton iter: Compute \(\eta\) share via k2ComputeEtaShareDS. threshold \(k\): compute \(\theta_k - \eta\) share via affine-combine (plaintext \(\theta_k\) public). Apply exp + recip \(\theta_k - \eta\) -> \(F_k\) share (\(F(u) = 1/(1+\exp(-u)) = e^u/(1+e^u)\) – evaluated exp(u) * (1/(1+exp(u))) via existing primitives). \(f_k = F_k (1 - F_k)\) via Beaver vecmul. Per-patient residual numerator/denominator built indicator-weighted differences (done outcome server since holds \(y_i\) plaintext). .ring127_recip_round_keyed F-difference share. Beaver vecmul (f-diff) * (1/F-diff) -> \(T_i\) share. Beaver matvec \(X^\top T\) -> aggregate score \(\beta\). Client Newton stacked \((\beta, \theta)\) using aggregate finite-difference Hessian share-domain scores.

title: K>=3 Ring63 DCF + Beaver Gradient Loop — k3-ring63-dcf-loop
text: Protocol: One-time: servers share features+y 2 DCF parties (Ring63 FP) Per iteration: DCF parties compute eta X_share*beta DCF wide spline DCF parties -> mu shares Beaver matvec DCF parties -> gradient shares Client aggregates Ring63 shares -> plaintext gradient Client L-BFGS update Requires: transport keys . CPK, Galois, RLK. Precision: ~1e-3 (DCF approximation).

title: dsVertClient (development version)
text: ds.vertCox — federated Cox proportional-hazards regression (reverse-cumsum reformulation, Allison 1982 / Andreux 2020). Supports left-truncation (tstart_col), stratification (strata_col), Path B damped fixed-Fisher refinement, ring = 63 | 127. Defaults Ring127 (5/5 STRICT Pima synthetic; ~2× faster Path B). Historical K=3 Cox Poisson-trick prototype offset + baseline dummies evaluated, removed product API favour non-disclosive profile route. ds.vertCoxDiscreteNonDisclosive — K=2 discrete-time pooled-logistic Cox Aliasgari-Blanton 2013 share-mask gating. ds.vertMultinom — K-class one-vs-rest multinomial logistic. ds.vertMultinomJoint — compatibility wrapper joint Newton softmax route. ds.vertMultinomJointNewton — full softmax Newton via Ring127 Chebyshev exp + reciprocal + Beaver vecmul per-patient eta_k / mu_k shares (paper §V.row, Bohning-bounded). ds.vertOrdinal — proportional-odds via cumulative-binomial BLUE pool + threshold correction (McCullagh-Agresti). ds.vertOrdinalJointNewton — Tutz 1990 §3.2 block-diagonal joint Newton McCullagh §2.5 closed-form H_θθ + post-Newton refinement; sub-noise close-well L2 K=2. ds.vertNB — negative binomial GLM via two-stage Poisson β + θ Newton-Raphson; joint = TRUE re-Poisson-fits theta-adjusted weights. ds.vertNBMoMTheta — closed-form Method--Moments theta (Anscombe 1950 / Saha-Paul 2005), psi-free. ds.vertNBFullRegTheta — first-order corrected full-regression theta Ring127 NR-LOG share-domain primitive (Goldschmidt 1964 + Pugh 2004); SUB-NOISE K=2 (105×–109× σ-probe ratio). ds.vertLMM — Laird-Ware GLS closed-form REML; supports random intercept + slopes via random_slopes; ring = ring127 pipeline STRICT closure dense Gram matrices. ds.vertLMM.k3 — REML 1-D profile LMM K=3. ds.vertGLMM — aggregate GLMM-PQL binomial mixed model (single random intercept) guarded share-domain cluster sufficient statistics; older EM/Laplace-style route diagnostics-. ds.vertIPW — propensity-weighted two-stage GLM wrapper. ds.vertLASSOProximal — proper LASSO via client-side proximal-gradient (FISTA-accelerated default) normal equations; 4–10× tighter coefficient agreement vs post-hoc soft-threshold. ds.vertLASSOIter — standardized L1 path Gaussian, binomial, Poisson GLMs; binomial uses aggregate-score FISTA LASSO-specific 200-interval secure sigmoid, Poisson uses aggregate score/Hessian prox-Newton. ds.vertLASSO1Step + ds.vertLASSOCV — one-step quadratic-surrogate LASSO AIC / BIC / EBIC information-criterion λ selector. ds.vertGEE — generalised estimating equations (working exchangeable / AR1 correlation, sandwich SE) per-call binomial_sigmoid_intervals precision control protected binomial sigmoid evaluations. ds.vertMI — multiple-imputation wrapper Rubin pooling. ds.vertChisq — two-way contingency χ² via Beaver dot product one-hot shares. ds.vertDesc — descriptive aggregates (mean / SD / min / max / histogram-based quantiles). ds.vertCor — Pearson correlation (Ring63 Beaver). ds.vertContrast, ds.vertWald, ds.vertConfint, ds.vertLR — inferential scaffolding (multivariate Wald, likelihood-ratio, contrast tests; CI helper).
